subject: they all did this...
body:
Hey Justin - Liam here.

Yesterday I hosted a free webinar with over 700 people.

And I talked about one of the biggest mistakes I see people make when they enter the AI space.

Most jump straight to the end goal.

They want to build an AI software company.
They want to be a full AI transformation partner.
They want to teach, educate, and position themselves as experts.

Big ideas. Ambitious goals.

But time and time again, they all run into the same wall.

When you look at the most successful people inside the Accelerator, there is a clear pattern.

Mohan has built an AI software company scaling past 2M ARR.
Mert and Arda built an AI transformation firm doing 400k+ in the last 6 months.
Nate Herk went on to build one of the strongest personal brands in AI education.

But here is the part most people miss:

That is not where any of them started.

They all began in the same place - by building an AI agency first.

They learned the fundamentals by doing the work:
How the technology actually works
How to land and work with AI clients
How to market in a crowded AI landscape
How to build solutions that create real, measurable impact

Too many people try to walk before they can run.

If you are planning to get into AI this year, I strongly recommend following the same path:
Start by building solutions for real clients.
Solve real problems.
Create real change.

Talk soon,
Liam
